rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHIKO KAWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of the co-administrators' failure to address any issue raised in the tentative ruling for the 6/28/21 hearing. The issue of the co-administrators' failure to fully insure the estate funds was initially raised in the tentative ruling for the 4/12/21 hearing, but no proof of observance of the FDIC limit has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WYMAN SETO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/29/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Although the amended petition was file stamped on 8/4/21, the day it was uploaded, it was not actually E-filed (and available for viewing) until 8/18/21 due to the current shortage of clerks. Therefore, notice mailed or published before 8/18/21 is defective. Are Hoa Tai and Michelle Tan the current co-trustees of the devisee trust or the "fourth named co-successor trustee[s]" of the trust? Item 8 of the amended petition is not very clear. If they are not the current co-trustees, the current trustee or co-trustees must be clearly identified. If the current trustee is the petitioner, all beneficiaries of the devisee trust must be clearly identified. Petitioner must address this in a verified supplement to the amended petition, prepared on pleading paper. Notice to a minor must be given directly to the minor (who is at least 12 years old) and also to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] (Item 3f(4) should not have been checked as no personal representative has been appointed for this estate. Also, notice to the state agencies must be given by the personal representative or her attorney, meaning after she is appointed, so that the agencies know to whom a claim should be addressed, not before a personal representative is appointed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/20/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROSEANNE BERTUCELLI AS TRUSTEE OF THE I TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Relief For Breach Of Trust; Breach Of Trust Trustee De Son Tort/De Facto Trustee; Complaint For Fraudulent Concealment **Filing Date Backdated To 4/1/21 Per Ex Parte Order Filed 5/19/21**
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/4/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The proof of service (no courtesy copy received by the Examiner, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.) is incomplete at item 4 (date and place of mailing). A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a).] It does not appear that notice was given to the person who is reportedly the current acting successor trustee. The petition caption does not disclose all relief sought. [CRC 7.102.] All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. Part Third of the trust requires the share of each child of the trustor to be held in trust during the lifetime of each child and then distributed to the children of the deceased child. It appears that an amendment to the petition must be filed, amending the petition caption and setting forth the last known address of Paula Bennett (or attaching her written direction to send notice to her attorney only), identifying any children of the current beneficiaries and stating their last known addresses, and also setting forth the last known address of Peter Renstrom. Notice of the amendment must be given after the amendment is filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 9/23/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
